description_long: This is a weathered, single-story wooden cabin with a large, rusted gable roof extending over a wide front porch. The facade features three rectangular windows and a single wooden door, all framed in dark wood, with the porch supported by simple posts. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden planks, showing signs of age and exposure. The roof is heavily rust-stained, suggesting corrugated metal construction, and overhangs the front and rear for added shelter. On one side, two additional windows are visible, while the rear and opposite side are windowless, emphasizing a utilitarian design. The overall appearance is rustic and functional, suitable for rural or edge-of-urban environments.
description: A rustic, weathered wooden cabin with a large rusted gable roof, wide front porch, and simple rectangular windows and door.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide porch, three windows, and a single door beneath the overhanging gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the rusted roof, with the porch edge and one supporting post visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain wooden wall with no windows or doors, and the full span of the gable roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with two rectangular windows and the roof overhang, no porch visible.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with significant overhangs, constructed from rusted corrugated metal
attribute_window_arrangement: Five rectangular windows: three symmetrically spaced on the front facade, two on one side wall, all of similar size and shape, with no windows on the rear or opposite side
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door located on the right side of the front facade, adjacent to the porch
